semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all twelve assigned source notes and their limits, every source mapping, schemas and fixtures, plan-execution/handoff/replacement/one-shot probes, the reachable typed vertical model, its executed nine-scenario and 89-event consumer, 30 concrete semantic-source mutations, four bounded empirical campaign generations and their immutable transitions, the revised proof surface, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, figures, lifecycle states, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made consumer-relative semantic and authority conformance across the accepted-contract lineage the distinct owner; separated intake, planning, compilation, runtime enforcement, artifact lineage, evaluation, release, and rights owners; added observed-effect, denominator, cost, expiry, recovery, causal, replication, and transfer gates; physically retired two assumption-restating declarations; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Accepted intent receipts lowered through commands, plans, jobs, adapter requests, observed effects, artifacts, verification, delivery, feedback, compensation, and residual custody for declared consumers and purposes.
scope.environment: Twelve reviewed assigned source notes; synthetic schema and route fixtures; handoff, replacement, and one-shot action probes; a reachable typed vertical model; an executed nine-scenario, 89-event consumer with three releases, three pre-effect refusals, two exact-rollback refusals, one failed-rollback quarantine, and 30 of 30 concrete semantic-source mutations rejected; one 16-run governed-work campaign; one 36-transaction usefulness campaign; one failed 12-task final-contract campaign; one repaired 32-candidate renewal; and a future natural multi-model conformance campaign. No production intent-to-effect service is represented.
scope.model: A versioned consumer-relative conformance relation over semantic obligations, authority, parties, state, means, effects, artifacts, verification, resources, stops, expiry, lineage, recovery, and residuals; current implementations include a reachable typed vertical transition model and an independent concrete-schema consumer over bounded executed repository-change traces, alongside trusted-field validators, finite routes, and bounded local campaign policies. They are not a natural-language semantic compiler or deployed enforcement system.
scope.authority: Human Intent owns interpretation and acceptance; governance and authority owners issue constraints and capabilities; planning, compilation, labor, runtime, security, artifact, verification, evidence, readiness, resource, rights, licensing, and publication owners retain their exact surfaces. This chapter can block, narrow, request re-contract, and emit evidence, but cannot grant authority, support, or release.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-15; intent, contract, policy, state, environment, dependency, evaluator, authority, budget, stop, criterion, publication, and outcome-horizon versions expire after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; twelve reviewed source mappings; intent/command/trace schemas; synthetic fixtures and results; the reachable vertical refinement model, executed consumer result, receipt, adequacy dossier, and 30 concrete mutations; campaign records and transitions; seven proof targets and three Lean modules; future natural task, contract, lineage, effect, evaluation, cost, recovery, causal, replication, and transfer bundles.

assumptions: An accepted intent receipt can identify consumer, purpose, objective, non-goals, parties, constraints, authority, state assumptions, means, criteria, stops, and residual duties without claiming complete interpretation.
A typed conformance relation can make material semantic loss and authority amplification observable without equating syntax or digests with meaning.
Natural public-safe tasks can expose interpretation, lowering, effect, artifact, delivery, useful-throughput, unsafe-release, cost, rights, and recovery differences under matched conditions.
Independent semantic, effect, artifact, and outcome observers can be implemented and their shared dependencies measured.
Strong human-authored, direct, schema-only, workflow, capability, and governed comparators can be matched on candidates, authority, models, tools, data, budgets, and horizons.
Normative choices about authority, affected parties, acceptable loss, privacy, rights, compensation, and publication remain explicit premises.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Typed APIs, mature workflow engines, capability systems, approval services, provenance/event logs, test suites, and ordinary human review already provide adequate end-to-end discipline; a separate conformance layer adds brittle schemas, duplicated state, abstention, latency, cost, false confidence, and missed help while remaining unable to formalize natural-language meaning or guarantee wise authority.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable ordinary or adversarial trace violates any semantic, authority, state, effect, artifact, verifier, denominator, expiry, recovery, or residual rule; a required field has no exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence; the conformance relation accepts semantically wrong work; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the preregistered joint frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current planning, workflow, capability, injection-control, specification, verification, provenance, and author-lineage comparators without importing reported performance or safety.
Justify consumer, purpose, authority, affected parties, acceptable semantic loss, rights, privacy, compensation, abstention, and publication boundaries.
Specify typed semantic obligations, trace refinement, authority and effect state machines, expiry, recovery, and countermodels beyond trusted Booleans and assumption-restating predicates.
Run actual parsers/lowerers, observed-state gates, capability enforcement, effect observers, artifact evaluators, delivery and feedback paths, compensation, and residual custody end to end.

Measure parseability, semantic fidelity, authority precision/recall, effect correctness, artifact satisfaction, useful delivery, unsafe release, abstention, missed help, delayed harm, rights, latency, resources, human work, recovery, and total governance cost jointly.
Ablate field semantics, data/control separation, authority gates, effect observation, verifier separation, expiry, denominator accounting, and recovery under matched candidates and histories.
Repeat across models, languages, modalities, tasks, runtimes, tools, organizations, jurisdictions, threat models, affected parties, and time with independent implementations.

contrary_evidence: Planning languages, workflow engines, capability security, event sourcing, provenance systems, tests, and human review already implement substantial pieces without a distinct conformance layer.
Natural-language intent and semantic equivalence may remain too contextual, contested, or open-world for a finite contract to capture without harmful false precision.
The current finite proofs trust field predicates and do not validate semantics, observed effects, evaluator correctness, or runtime refinement.
The 16-run campaign released nothing; the 36-transaction campaign found only two correct candidates and four inexact attack-control rollbacks.
The first 12-task natural campaign produced zero parseable final contracts.
The repaired 32-candidate renewal produced only two correct candidates, zero useful or unsafe releases in either arm, and a prospectively frozen metadata error.
Governance and schema burden can increase latency, abstention, cost, operator load, false denial, and missed help without improving useful output.
Separate evaluators and observers may share models, data, infrastructure, incentives, blind spots, or authority with the system under review.
known_confounds: Human-authored reference contracts and semantic labels can be incomplete, inconsistent, culturally narrow, or outcome-informed.
Governed routes may receive more tokens, calls, repair, tools, or human hints than baselines, or may avoid difficult tasks and appear safer through non-release.
Field-completeness, digest equality, route coverage, effect receipts, and exact local rollback can be mistaken for semantic fidelity, safety, or production recovery.
Parsers, generators, evaluators, and observers can share model families, training data, prompts, code, or infrastructure despite distinct process identities.
Failed parses, abstentions, retries, discarded candidates, blocked effects, rollback failures, and unresolved deliveries can be removed from denominators.
Short outcome horizons can miss delayed, indirect, descendant, privacy, rights, and compensation effects.
Governance latency, operator work, opportunity cost, false denial, missed help, storage, privacy, recovery, and maintenance can be omitted or shifted outside the campaign.
Frozen task-family and attack metadata can disagree with exact files, as observed in the renewal campaign.
acceptance_criteria: Every semantic, authority, state, effect, artifact, verifier, denominator, budget, expiry, recovery, and residual field has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid case, rejecting mutation, and observed consequence.

A preregistered natural multi-model campaign meets every joint interpretation, conformance, authority, effect, artifact, useful-delivery, unsafe-release, abstention, cost, rights, recovery, causal, replication, and transfer gate against strong matched comparators.

Each claimed mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by extra resources, hidden human repair, weak baselines, evaluator dependence, denominator changes, or non-release.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact consumer, purpose, task, language, modality, model, parser, contract version, authority, state, environment, tool, evaluator, observer, budget, threat, horizon, observed effects, costs, and residuals.

Separate parseability, field presence, semantic fidelity, authority validity, dispatch, observed effect, artifact satisfaction, delivery, usefulness, unsafe release, abstention, recovery, support movement, and live authority.

Unknown interpretation, affected-party coverage, semantic loss, authority, observer independence, external effect, delayed outcome, rights, compensation, replication, or transfer remains residual, blocked, narrowed, or refuted.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.

Refute a mechanism benefit when its matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by extra resources, human repair, weak baselines, denominator change, or non-release.

Refute reliable, safer, useful, superior, general, independent, reproducible, or transferable execution language when a simpler comparator dominates, semantics or authority drift, effects escape, usefulness remains zero, recovery fails, or replication/transfer fails.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise intent, planning, compilation, labor, runtime, security, artifact, verification, evidence, resource, rights, or release owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and execute the natural multi-model conformance campaign with human-authored, direct, schema-only, strong workflow/capability, and governed routes; independent semantic/effect/artifact/outcome observers; identical candidates and authority; nonzero useful opportunity; complete costs and residuals; delayed outcomes; effect-complete recovery; causal ablations; replication; and transfer.

non_claims: A typed contract, complete field set, digest, route theorem, dispatch receipt, effect acknowledgement, graph, validator, exact local rollback, zero release, or green status does not establish semantic fidelity, wise authority, useful execution, safety, or production transfer.
The current fixtures, mutations, 19 theorem declarations, and four campaign generations establish only their exact records, finite consequences, bounded outcomes, and no-promotion boundaries.
No intent, plan, tool, effect, artifact, support state, deployment, publication, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted transition.

intent-to-execution-contracts.core.strongest_counterclaim: Existing typed workflow, capability, provenance, and review systems are adequate; the proposed relation cannot formalize meaning and adds costly failure surfaces.
intent-to-execution-contracts.core.falsifier: Any accepted trace loses a material obligation or authority bound, a semantically wrong result passes, or a strong simpler comparator dominates the joint frontier.
acceptance_criteria: Meet every preregistered natural conformance, usefulness, safety, cost, recovery, causal, replication, and transfer gate against strong comparators.

intent-to-execution-contracts.minimum.00.strongest_counterclaim: The record suite already constitutes a useful implementation even without semantic parsing or production effects.
intent-to-execution-contracts.minimum.00.falsifier: Any current artifact is presented as end-to-end semantic, authority, effect, usefulness, safety, or production evidence beyond its exact route or outcome.
acceptance_criteria: Execute the frozen natural multi-model campaign with independent observers, nonzero useful opportunity, effect-bearing controls, causal ablations, replication, and transfer.

intent-to-execution-contracts.beyond_sot.strongest_counterclaim: The full governed route is dominated by simpler systems and adds abstention, cost, latency, false denial, and false confidence.
intent-to-execution-contracts.beyond_sot.falsifier: Any joint gate fails, mechanism signatures do not survive ablation, simpler baselines dominate, or independent replication and transfer fail.
acceptance_criteria: Complete the multi-comparator causal, adversarial, independently implemented, replicated, and transferred program and meet every joint gate.

intent-to-execution-contracts.invariant..strongest_counterclaim: Low-risk effects can safely use contextual inference and revocable defaults.
intent-to-execution-contracts.invariant..falsifier: Any inferred, defaulted, stale, conflicting, or missing authority causes a material effect without separate authorization.
acceptance_criteria: Inject each authority weakness across real effect classes and observe denial before effect with bounded false-denial cost.

intent-to-execution-contracts.invariant..strongest_counterclaim: For bounded local tasks, dispatch success plus validator acceptance is a sufficient proxy for completion.
intent-to-execution-contracts.invariant..falsifier: Any earlier state is narrated or consumed as a later state, or independently observed effects and delivery do not change the decision.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise request-through-usefulness traces with acknowledgements, partial effects, wrong artifacts, failed delivery, compensation, and delayed outcomes.

intent-to-execution-contracts.invariant..strongest_counterclaim: Zero release under adversarial tasks is itself meaningful safety evidence.
intent-to-execution-contracts.invariant..falsifier: A preregistered analysis with nonzero independently useful opportunity validly estimates safety benefit from abstention without usefulness or unsafe-release distortion.
acceptance_criteria: Require nonzero independently useful candidates and matched unsafe-release opportunity before claiming safer useful execution.

intent-to-execution-contracts.failure_mo.strongest_counterclaim: One composite acceptance status is operationally necessary and can encode all subsidiary checks.
intent-to-execution-contracts.failure_mo.falsifier: A composite status preserves every subsidiary uncertainty, disagreement, denominator, cost, and residual without changing decisions or reader claims.
acceptance_criteria: Mutate each evidence lane independently and verify that outcomes and public claims change without being hidden by aggregate green state.

intent-to-execution-contracts.failure_mo.strongest_counterclaim: Because the chapter explicitly reports abstention, fail-closed theater is already controlled.
intent-to-execution-contracts.failure_mo.falsifier: A dated audit finds no surface that treats zero release, blocked work, or exact local rollback as usefulness, safety advantage, or production recovery.
acceptance_criteria: Audit every chapter, appendix, synopsis, and release surface under zero-useful and zero-unsafe denominators.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes the preservation predicate and concludes its consequent, so it proves no compiler or semantic property.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.falsifier: It is presented as evidence that an actual compiler preserves constraints or lacks an explicit activation-only consumer.
acceptance_criteria: Replace or refine with typed source/target semantics, a compiler relation, countermodels, validated producers, and runtime correspondence.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes missing approval blocks running and models neither approval validity nor runtime enforcement.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.falsifier: It is cited as approval-service or effect-enforcement evidence, or a forged/stale approval satisfies the model.
acceptance_criteria: Refine against typed grants, observed state, expiry, revocation, one-shot use, effect receipts, and an executable enforcement path.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.strongest_counterclaim: Trusted Booleans prove only branch order and cannot validate field meaning, authority, effects, or completeness.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.falsifier: A semantically invalid or forged record reaches dispatchReady, or prose calls route coverage dispatcher safety.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed producers and trace semantics to effect-bearing dispatch and mutate semantic, authority, state, observer, and residual failures.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem trusts summary bits and proves nothing about the handoff trace or its digests.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.int.falsifier: A malformed or semantically wrong trace can set the summary bits or the theorem is cited as vertical execution evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Connect typed trace objects, exact digest derivation, observed effects, independent artifact evaluation, and natural workload replay.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.com.strongest_counterclaim: Five true presence bits do not establish meaningful, unambiguous, authorized, or testable fields.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.com.falsifier: Vague or contradictory fields satisfy the predicate or it is presented as command validity.
acceptance_criteria: Model units, quantifiers, provenance, precedence, ambiguity, authority, consumers, verifiers, and consequences with counterexamples.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.com.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes the explicit-precedence predicate and does not detect hidden instructions or enforce capability boundaries.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.com.falsifier: It is cited as prompt-injection resistance or a hidden instruction bypasses an executable producer.
acceptance_criteria: Refine against typed trusted control, untrusted data, policy extraction, tool capabilities, attack corpora, and observed effects.

intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.com.strongest_counterclaim: Hard-coded confidence enums and dispatch eligibility are policy declarations, not calibrated uncertainty or semantic evidence.
intent-to-execution-contracts.formal.com.falsifier: Misclassified or stale confidence reaches dispatch, or the route is cited as parser-quality evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Measure confidence calibration, provenance errors, authority consequences, false denial, and effect outcomes on natural tasks.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.6910.rationale: Human-path wording restates the no-ambient-authority invariant.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.5c9e.rationale: Human-path explanation restates the contract obligations.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.8125.rationale: Reader explanation of the authority boundary.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.cfe0.rationale: Restates the chapter's distinct conformance owner.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.31ed.rationale: Reports the exact current fixture and proof ceiling.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.ff3b.rationale: Restates missing or inferred authority blocking.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.8c60.rationale: Restates exercised producer, consumer, mutation, and consequence requirements.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.d328.rationale: Reports the synthetic replacement bridge route.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.12c2.rationale: Explicit non-claim for the bridge fixture.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.2a03.rationale: Reports the bounded post-v2 campaign limit.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.772b.rationale: Reports the post-v2.1 campaign design and denominator.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.6bad.rationale: Reports the post-v2.3 final-contract failure interpretation.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.56f7.rationale: Summary describes the present artifact ceiling.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.9792.rationale: Summary states the central negative campaign result.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.3b4a.rationale: Summary restates future causal and transfer gates.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.155b.rationale: The bounded reachable-model result is already owned by the strengthened operational-invariant formal target.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.a0b8.rationale: States the exact formal and executable evidence limitations and makes no new positive claim.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.c00c.rationale: Continues the explicit non-claim boundary for production, reproduction, transfer, and chapter-core support.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.a62a.rationale: A wrapped result-description fragment restates the stricter authority-confidence and receipt requirements already owned by the command semantic-interface formal targets.

intent-to-execution-contracts.prose.06ec.rationale: Line-wrapped mature-endpoint or current-result boundary already governed by structured chapter atoms; it adds no independent material claim.

non_claims: This review does not promote the chapter above argument or convert schema, fixture, theorem, campaign, exact local rollback, abstention, or no-change records into semantic, safety, or production evidence.

This review does not establish correct intent interpretation, legitimate authority, semantic equivalence, prompt-injection resistance, approval enforcement, safe tools, useful execution, verifier independence, effect-complete recovery, production transfer, AGI, or ASI.
No external-human prepublication review is required or claimed, and no external publication or release is authorized by this packet.
